item.title: Power Company ERock Seeks to Raise $642 Million in IPO
item.description: ERock Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $642 million from an initial public offering, joining a rush of listings ahead of SpaceX later this month.

item.title: Dollar Opens Week Boosted by AI Enthusiasm, Haven Demand
item.description: Traders are betting the US dollar will rise in the weeks ahead, supported by lingering uncertainty over US-Iran talks and an AI-fueled rally in US stocks.

item.title: IBM Shares Surge as Old Clip of Trump Praise Recirculates Online
item.description: A nearly six-month old video of Donald Trump praising International Business Machines Corp.’s CEO sent the shares surging almost 9% on Monday, adding more than $24 billion to the company’s stock-market value.

item.title: FedEx Freight Shares Drop in First Day of Trading After Spinoff
item.description: Shares of FedEx Corp.’s former freight division fell on Monday in their first day of trading as an independent, publicly held company.

item.title: US Manufacturing Activity Expands by Most in Four Years
item.description: US manufacturing activity expanded in May at the fastest pace in four years, bolstered by a pickup in new orders and production.

item.title: Peru Inflation Unexpectedly Slows, Still Running Over Target
item.description: Inflation in Peru’s capital came in below expectations in May, but remained above the central bank’s target range for the third straight month.

item.title: US Extends Deadline for Trilogy Metals Equity Stake Purchase
item.description: Trilogy Metals Inc. said the US is giving the critical minerals miner more time to finalize the Pentagon’s planned $35.6 million equity investment in the company.

item.title: US Bonds Drop as Doubt Over US-Iran Talks Lifts Oil Prices
item.description: Treasuries sank as an impasse in negotiations between the US and Iran fueled concern that higher energy costs will stoke inflation and push the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

item.title: Investors Pick Up Bargains in Europe’s Embattled Software Sector
item.description: Fund managers are snapping up beaten-down European software stocks as fears of disruption from artificial intelligence fade.

item.title: Apollo’s Slok Says AI Will Dash Warsh’s Hopes for Quick Rate Cut
item.description: The build-out for artificial intelligence will be inflationary in the early going, preventing new Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh from cutting interest rates as quickly as he has suggested should be possible, according to Torsten Slok of Apollo Global Management Inc.

item.title: General Dynamics Will Spend $200 Million to Reboot Ammo Plant
item.description: General Dynamics Corp. will invest $200 million of its own money and unwind a partnership with Turkish defense contractor Repkon in a bid to finally start producing 155mm artillery shells at a Texas plant that’s been beset by delays, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Revolut Vows to Hire 200 More France Staff in Europe Growth Push
item.description: Revolut Ltd. plans to add another 200 employees in France as the UK fintech continues its European expansion.

item.title: Chile Economy Starts Second Quarter With Minimal Monthly Growth
item.description: Chile’s economic activity barely rose at the start of the second quarter, the central bank reported on Monday, marking the latest in a series of disappointing indicators in one of Latin America’s richest nations.

item.title: Honeywell-Backed Quantinuum Boosts IPO Target to $1.46 Billion
item.description: Honeywell International Inc.-backed quantum computing company Quantinuum Inc. boosted the size of its initial public offering to as much as $1.46 billion, raising the number of shares offered and the price range.

item.title: Turkey Is in Talks With Russia on Gas Supplies Beyond 2026
item.description: Turkey and Russia are discussing an extension of natural gas supply agreements beyond 2026 as current deals expire at the end of the year.

item.title: African Startup Spiro Raises $215 Million, Nearing Unicorn Value
item.description: African electric-mobility startup Spiro raised $215 million backed by European and African investors as it nears $1 billion in value, or so-called unicorn status, founder Gagan Gupta said.

item.title: Saylor’s Strategy Follows Through on Pledge to Sell Some Bitcoin
item.description: Strategy Inc., the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, sold about $2.5 million of the cryptocurrency, marking its first disposal of the cryptocurrency since 2022.

item.title: QXO Kicks Off $3 Billion Loan Sale in M&A Debt Deal for TopBuild
item.description: A group of banks led by Wells Fargo & Co. kicked off a $3 billion leveraged loan sale as part of a larger debt package to finance building products distributor QXO Building Products Inc.’s purchase of rival TopBuild Corp.

item.title: Activist Toms Capital Ramps Up Campaign at Voya Financial
item.description: Insurance company Voya Financial Inc. is facing increased pressure from activist investor Toms Capital Investment Management to take M&A action and make changes.

item.title: US Futures Rise as Traders Remain Hopeful of US-Iran Peace Deal
item.description: US stocks headed for a positive open on Monday as traders remained hopeful that Washington and Tehran would strike a peace deal, even amid a rise in oil prices.

item.title: Chile Investors Turn Back to Peso Notes as If War Never Happened
item.description: As Washington inches closer to a deal with Iran, Chilean investors are moving back into short-term peso bonds, wagering that inflation fears sparked by the Middle East conflict are a thing of the past.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 1, 2026
item.description: US equity-index futures rise, despite higher oil prices. Contracts on the S&P 500 Index are up 0.3% at 7:46 a.m. in New York.

item.title: JPMorgan Traders Cast Doubt on Speculative Tech After 57% Rally
item.description: The riskiest corners of the tech sector are outperforming their larger peers at the fastest pace in nearly six years. Now, many on Wall Street are sounding a warning to investors holding those stocks: Get out while you can.

item.title: India’s Factory Output Expands 4.9% Under Revised Data Series
item.description: India’s factory output grew 4.9% in April from a year earlier under a revised data series designed to better reflect structural changes in Asia’s third-largest economy.

item.title: India May Scale Back Some UK FTA Concessions Over Steel Duties
item.description: India may reconsider some concessions under the free trade agreement with the UK if its steel exports do not receive relief from the proposed safeguard duties, a senior government official in New Delhi said.

item.title: Jefferies Sees Turkish Case for Argentina-Style Dollar Swap Line
item.description: The US could potentially offer a dollar swap line to Turkey ahead of the election, a move that will bolster hard-currency reserves and improve market confidence, according to Jefferies International strategist Durukal Gun.

item.title: Colombian Bonds Surge as Right-Wing Outsider Moves Into Runoff
item.description: Colombia’s dollar bonds rallied Monday after right-wing outsider Abelardo de La Espriella unexpectedly won the first-round of voting for president and went into the runoff as clear favorite against leftist Ivan Cepeda.

item.title: EDP Said to Start €200 Million Stake Sale of Iberia Solar Assets
item.description: Portuguese utility EDP SA is set to start the process to sell a minority stake in its Iberian distributed-generation assets, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Trump May Keep Copper Traders Guessing on Tariffs
item.description: The president’s economic goals might be best served by continued ambiguity on import levies.

item.title: South Africa Gasoline Prices Hit Record After Relief Rollback
item.description: South African gasoline prices are set to reach a record high this month after the government rolled back some temporary relief measures that had cushioned consumers against fuel-cost increases sparked by the Iran war.
